headline: Supermicro shares surge 20% as robust margins, $60B backlog eclipse soft revenue
summary: Investing.com -- Shares of Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) rocketed 20% in after-hours trading on Tuesday, driven by a blockbuster preliminary fourth-quarter update that showcased record-breaking demand and a massive leap in profitability. Despite projecting top-line revenue near the lower end of its expected range, the server and AI infrastructure giant completely reset the market’s profitability narrative. headline: Super Micro sees strong margins, books $60 billion in orders; shares jump
summary: Super Micro Computer said on Tuesday it had secured more than $60 billion in ‌new orders in the fourth quarter, and ‌now expects gross margin to exceed its previous forecast, sending its ​shares surging 17.5% in extended trading.  Artificial-intelligence infrastructure firms have seen demand accelerate as tech companies and cloud providers ramp up investments in data centers to support large ‌language models and other ⁠AI applications.  The AI server maker expects gross margins in the range of 15% to ⁠17% for the quarter ended June 30, well above its earlier forecast of 8.2% to 8.4%, "primarily due to ​a favorable ​customer and product mix."

The AI server maker expects gross margins in the range of 15% to ⁠17% for the quarter ended June 30, well above its earlier forecast of 8.2% to 8.4%, "primarily due to ​a favorable ​customer and product mix."

headline: Supermicro Provides Fourth Quarter of Fiscal Year 2026 Preliminary Business Update
summary: SAN JOSE, Calif., July 21, 2026--Super Micro Computer, Inc. (NASDAQ: SMCI) ("Supermicro" or the "Company"), an AI, Enterprise, Storage, and 5G/Edge IT Total Solution Provider, featuring Data Center Building Block Solutions® (DCBBS), today is providing a preliminary business update for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026 (Q4’26), ended June 30, 2026, and schedules earnings call for August 11th at 5pm EDT.

headline: Cloud Infrastructure Leaders and Laggards Widen as Market Flocks to Cash Flow Giants
summary: The data center theme shows a sharp divide: strong fundamentals with 66% ROIC but market selectivity. NVIDIA leads with 125% ROIC, while Super Micro and IBM struggle with cash flow and debt issues.

headline: 1 Cash-Burning Stock to Own for Decades and 2 Facing Challenges
summary: Companies that burn cash at a rapid pace can run into serious trouble if they fail to secure funding. Without a clear path to profitability, these businesses risk dilution, mounting debt, or even bankruptcy.
